channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: H-Probes: Extracting Hierarchical Structures From Latent Representations of Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.00847v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Representing and navigating hierarchy is a fundamental primitive of reasoning. Large language models have demonstrated proficiency in a wide variety of tasks requiring hierarchical reasoning, but there exists limited analysis on how the models geometr...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, analysis, reasoning, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Model Organisms Are Leaky: Perplexity Differencing Often Reveals Finetuning Objectives
item.description: arXiv:2605.00994v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Finetuning can significantly modify the behavior of large language models, including introducing harmful or unsafe behaviors. To study these risks, researchers develop model organisms: models finetuned to exhibit specific known behaviors for controlle...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, RAG, API, experiment, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Can AI Debias the News? LLM Interventions Improve Cross-Partisan Receptivity but LLMs Overestimate Their Own Effectiveness
item.description: arXiv:2605.01006v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Partisan news media erode cross-partisan trust, but large language models (LLMs) offer a potential means of debiasing such content at scale. Across two pre-registered experiments, we tested whether LLM-generated debiasing of liberal news headlines cou...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, study, arxiv, large language model, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Psychologically Potent, Computationally Invisible: LLMs Generate Social-Comparison Triggers They Fail to Detect
item.description: arXiv:2605.01017v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We introduce Xiaohongshu Social Comparison Reader Elicitation (XHS-SCoRE), a reader-grounded benchmark for detecting if a text-only Xiaohongshu (RedNote) post elicits UPWARD, DOWNWARD, or NEUTRAL/no clear social comparison from a first-person reader p...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, prompt, study, framework, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Systematic Exploration of Text Decomposition and Budget Distribution in Differentially Private Text Obfuscation
item.description: arXiv:2605.01065v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The goal of differentially private text obfuscation is to obfuscate, or "perturb", input texts with Differential Privacy (DP) guarantees, such that the private output texts are quantifiably indistinguishable from the originals. While perturbation at t...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, reasoning, experiment, research, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Controlled Paraphrase Geometry in Sentence Embedding Space: Local Manifold Modeling and Latent Probing
item.description: arXiv:2605.01073v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The paper studies the local geometry of embedding clouds induced by \emph{controlled local classes of semantically close sentences}. The central question is how controlled paraphrase-like semantic variation is organized in sentence embedding space and...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, embedding, template, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Polynomial-Time Optimal Group Selection via the Double-Commutator Eigenvalue Problem
item.description: arXiv:2605.00834v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The algebraic diversity framework replaces temporal averaging over multiple observations with algebraic group action on a single observation for second-order statistical estimation. The central open problem in this framework is $\textit{group selectio...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, RAG, framework, arxiv, vector | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Linking spatial biology and clinical histology via Haiku
item.description: arXiv:2605.00925v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Integrating molecular, morphological, and clinical data is essential for basic and translational biomedical research, yet systematic frameworks for jointly modeling these modalities remain limited. Here we present Haiku, a tri-modal contrastive learni...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> zero-shot, context, analysis, embedding, retrieval | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: PhaseNet++: Phase-Aware Frequency-Domain Anomaly Detection for Industrial Control Systems via Phase Coherence Graphs
item.description: arXiv:2605.00929v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multivariate time series anomaly detection in ICS has attracted growing attention due to the increasing threat of cyber-physical attacks on critical infrastructure. State-of-the-art methods model inter-sensor relationships from raw time-domain amplitu...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, attention, study, transformer, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Agentic AI for Trip Planning Optimization Application
item.description: arXiv:2605.00276v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Trip planning for intelligent vehicles increasingly requires selecting optimal routes rather than merely producing feasible itineraries, as interacting factors such as travel time, energy consumption, and traffic conditions directly affect plan qualit...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, reasoning, ICL, experiment, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Sparse Regression under Correlation and Weak Signals: A Reproducible Benchmark of Classical and Bayesian Methods
item.description: arXiv:2605.00835v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Choosing between classical and Bayesian sparse regression methods involves a real trade-off: penalized estimators like Lasso run in milliseconds but give no uncertainty estimates,while Horseshoe and Spike-and-Slab priors produce full posteriors but ne...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, RAG, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Causal Foundations of Collective Agency
item.description: arXiv:2605.00248v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: A key challenge for the safety of advanced AI systems is the possibility that multiple simpler agents might inadvertently form a collective agent with capabilities and goals distinct from those of any individual. More generally, determining when a gro...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> study, framework, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: From Euler to Dormand-Prince: ODE Solvers for Flow Matching Generative Models
item.description: arXiv:2605.00836v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Sampling from Flow Matching generative models requires solving an ordinary differential equation (ODE) whose computational cost is dominated by neural network forward passes. We derive four classical ODE solvers -- Euler, Explicit Midpoint, Classical ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, ICL, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Review of the Receiver Operating Characteristic Curve and a Proof About the Area Beneath It
item.description: arXiv:2605.00926v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The Receiver Operating Characteristic (ROC) curve of a binary classifier has often been utilized to measure the performance of the classifier. The area beneath this curve is used in particular because of its quoted probabilistic interpretation as bein...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
